expect: Conflict resolution protocol: detect conflicting frontier claims, classify (duplicate/complementary/contradictory), propose resolution (merge/defer/split). >=3 conflict types with resolution rules.
actual: 4 conflict scopes (frontier/belief/philosophy/lane), 6 conflict types (DUPLICATE/COMPLEMENTARY/CONTRADICTORY/NEAR-DUPLICATE/DIVERGENT/ACTIVE-COLLISION + LOCAL-ONLY/PEER-ONLY), 7 resolution strategies. Full cmd_resolve() in swarm_peer.py with JSON report output.
diff: Expected >=3 types, got 6+2 types across 4 scopes. Exceeded. Unexpected: philosophy conflicts (asymmetric PHIL claims) and lane active-collision are distinct dimensions from frontier conflicts. Frontier classification uses shared-evidence heuristic (lesson ID overlap). Belief classification uses word-overlap similarity.
method: Added resolve command to swarm_peer.py. Clones peer, computes state fingerprints, classifies conflicts across 4 dimensions, proposes resolutions per type, saves JSON report to workspace/conflict-reports/.

conflict_types.DUPLICATE: Both resolved same frontier with shared evidence — merge-keep-richer
conflict_types.COMPLEMENTARY: One resolved/one active, or both active with independent evidence — adopt or split-parallel
conflict_types.CONTRADICTORY: Both claim resolution but different evidence — adversarial-review required
conflict_types.NEAR-DUPLICATE: Belief >70% word overlap — converged independently, merge-keep-newer
conflict_types.ACTIVE-COLLISION: Both swarms actively working same frontier — coordinate-or-split-scope
conflict_types.LOCAL-ONLY: PHIL claim exists locally but not in peer — propose-to-peer
conflict_types.PEER-ONLY: PHIL claim exists in peer but not locally — evaluate-for-adoption
